title: asperula cynanchica subsp. capillacea
intro: Asperula cynanchica subsp. capillacea, also known as the mountain woodrush, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the gentian family that grows in mountainous regions. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and soothing digestive issues, due to its rich content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. Herbal preparations of Asperula cynanchica subsp. capillacea include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to take advantage of its medicinal and health benefits.
